channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Grammarly Is Facing a Class Action Lawsuit Over Its AI ‘Expert Review’ Feature
item.description: The feature, which Grammarly shut down Wednesday, presented editing suggestions as if they came from established authors and academics—without their consent.

item.title: Welcome to Container Harbour! 🚢 Ep.15
item.description: Episode 15: Leaving Harbour Gracefully 🌅 The Production Deployment That Used to Require a 2am Saturday Maintenance Window 😤 Every engineer who has been around long enough has a war story. Mine...

item.title: Welcome to Container Harbour! 🚢 Ep.14
item.description: Episode 14: Reserved Berths for Divas — StatefulSets 🎭 The Database That Would NOT Behave Like a Normal Deployment 😤 We were young. We were naive. We thought: "It's just another container. Deploy...

item.title: Welcome to Container Harbour! 🚢 Ep.13
item.description: Episode 13: The Night Shift Nobody Talks About 🌙 The Cron Tab That Lived on ONE Server and Nobody Knew About It 😱 Every organisation has one. A server somewhere — maybe physical, maybe a VM,...

item.title: Welcome to Container Harbour! 🚢 Ep.12
item.description: Episode 12: Rush Hour at the Harbour — Autoscaling Under Pressure 📈 The Night Traffic Tripled and NOBODY Woke Up 🌙 3:47am. A major news outlet shared our link. Traffic went from 500...

item.title: Welcome to Container Harbour! 🚢 Ep.11
item.description: Episode 11: The Health Inspector Visits 🩺 The App That Was "Running" But Absolutely Not Running 😤 Here is a scenario I have personally lived through. The app is deployed. All Pods show 1/1...

item.title: Welcome to Container Harbour! 🚢 Ep.10
item.description: Episode 10: ID Badges and Security Guards — RBAC 🪪 The Engineer Who Gave EVERYONE cluster-admin 😭 True story. New Kubernetes cluster. Eager team lead who just wanted everyone to be productive....

item.title: Welcome to Container Harbour! 🚢 Ep.9
item.description: Episode 9: The Long-Term Warehouse — Persistent Volumes and Storage 🏭 The Database That Lost Its Mind When the Pod Restarted 😱 "My database keeps losing all its data every time I redeploy." I have...

item.title: Welcome to Container Harbour! 🚢 Ep.8
item.description: Episode 8: Sealed Cargo Manifests — ConfigMaps and Secrets 🔐 The Incident That Shall Not Be Named (But I'm Going to Name It) 😬 A junior developer — not me, definitely not me, I was definitely not...

item.title: Welcome to Container Harbour! 🚢 Ep.7
item.description: Episode 7: The Customs Office — Ingress and Smart Traffic Routing 🛃 We Had SEVENTEEN LoadBalancers. SEVENTEEN. 😱 I worked at a company once where every microservice had its own LoadBalancer...

item.title: Welcome to Container Harbour! 🚢 Ep.6
item.description: Episode 6: The Harbour Gates — Services and How Traffic Flows In 🚦 The Pod IP Address Problem Is Going to Make You CRY 😭 OK. Your Deployment is running. Three beautiful Pods. You're feeling good....

item.title: The Live Nation settlement has industry insiders baffled
item.description: Instead of moving forward with a jury trial against Live Nation-Ticketmaster as expected, the Justice Department announced a settlement Monday that omitted what used to be on the top of its wish...

item.title: Replit snags $9B valuation 6 months after hitting $3B
item.description: Replit raised a new $400 million round and said it hopes to have $1B in ARR by year's end.

item.title: Grammarly says it will stop using AI to clone experts without permission
item.description: Superhuman says it has disabled Grammarly's "expert review" AI feature that said its edit suggestions were "inspired by" real writers, including our editor-in-chief and other Verge staff members....

item.title: Microsoft’s ‘Xbox mode’ is coming to every Windows 11 PC
item.description: Microsoft seems more determined than ever to combine Xbox and Windows - to the point that its next-gen Xbox, codenamed Project Helix, will play PC games too. Today, we learned Helix will go alpha...

item.title: Nvidia Will Spend $26 Billion to Build Open-Weight AI Models, Filings Show
item.description: The move could position the AI infrastructure powerhouse to quickly compete with OpenAI, Anthropic, and DeepSeek.

item.title: Microsoft’s next Xbox, Project Helix, won’t reach alpha until 2027
item.description: We're here at the 2026 Game Developers Conference, where Microsoft "VP of Next Generation" Jason Ronald is talking about a topic near and dear to many gamers' hearts: the future of Xbox. Ronald...

item.title: OpenAI’s Sora video generator is reportedly coming to ChatGPT
item.description: OpenAI's Sora video generator could soon become a built-in feature in ChatGPT, as reported by The Information. Sora is currently only available on its website or as a standalone app, which has...

item.title: WordPress debuts a private workspace that runs in your browser via a new service, my.WordPress.net
item.description: WordPress’s new browser-based service lets users create private sites without hosting or signing up, turning the platform into a personal workspace for writing, research, and AI tools.

item.title: Researchers Trick Perplexity's Comet AI Browser Into Phishing Scam in Under Four Minutes
item.description: Agentic web browsers that leverage artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities to autonomously execute actions across multiple websites on behalf of a user could be trained and tricked into falling...

item.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
item.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News...

item.title: Rivian spin-out Mind Robotics raises $500M for industrial AI-powered robots
item.description: The startup, which was created by Rivian founder RJ Scaringe, is looking to train on data from, and deploy in, Rivian's factory.

item.title: Alternative app store AltStore PAL is joining the fediverse
item.description: AltStore PAL, the alternative iOS app store available in the European Union and Japan, is joining the social web. In a post on Wednesday, AltStore PAL announced that users across Mastodon,...

item.title: Code Concepts: A Large-Scale Synthetic Dataset Generated from Programming Concept Seeds
Meta’s Moltbook deal points to a future built around AI agents
item.description: Meta’s Moltbook acquisition may look odd at first, but the deal could signal how Meta sees AI agents shaping future advertising and commerce on an agentic web.

item.title: Canva’s new editing tool adds layers to AI-generated designs
item.description: Canva introduced a new feature that separates flat image files and AI-generated visuals into layered, fully editable designs. The Magic Layers tool is launching in public beta today in the US, UK,...

item.title: Critical n8n Flaws Allow Remote Code Execution and Exposure of Stored Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of two now-patched security flaws in the n8n workflow automation platform, including two critical bugs that could result in arbitrary command...

item.title: Amazon expands a program that lets customers shop from other retailers’ sites
item.description: The changes allow more merchants to participate in Amazon's Shop Direct program, which sends Amazon customers to other retailers' websites.

item.title: How to ditch Ring’s surveillance network
item.description: Recently, Ring ran a Super Bowl ad for its Search Party feature showing how it uses AI to scan footage from Ring cameras and video doorbells to help find lost dogs. It sounds neighborly — until...

item.title: You can’t replace the battery in Lego’s Smart Bricks — and many of its sensors aren’t available yet
item.description: The first Lego Smart Brick sets, based on Star Wars, aren't quite what my kids and I hoped, and I suspect much of that's down to programming. But the Smart Bricks may also have some technical...
